blocks.content: **Course Abstract:** Welcome to the foundational session of Google Antigravity (AGV). Before we can orchestrate AI agents to write code or execute terminal commands, we must establish a secure, working environment.

Based on our enterprise pilot sessions, **AGV-101 v0.2** focuses *exclusively* on getting Antigravity successfully installed and initialized within a restricted corporate IT environment. We will bypass Docker and external repository blocks by utilizing a secure Tarball method on a provisioned Cloudtop VM. As we like to say: *"No install, no magic."*
Instructor Note: Use this outline to create the introductory slide deck for the session. Present this before beginning the hands-on labs.
Slide 1: Welcome to Secure Agentic Development (L100)

items: **Visual:** Course title with high-tech, futuristic AGV branding focusing on "Installation & Setup".

**Talking Track:** "Welcome to Secure Agentic Development with Google Antigravity. Today, we are focusing on the critical first step: successfully installing and securing your AGV Mission Control in a locked-down corporate environment."
**Visual:** Diagram showing a developer at a laptop (Mission Control) orchestrating multiple AI agents (Workers).

**Talking Track:** "Google Antigravity is your Mission Control for agentic workflows. However, before agents can build for us, we need a functional, secure installation that abides by strict corporate guardrails."

blocks.content: Slide 3: The Corporate IT Environment & Installation Strategy

items: **Visual:** A warning icon next to a terminal window, transitioning to a terminal executing a 1-click install script.

**Talking Track:** "Corporate IT environments restrict Docker and external repositories. To bypass these limitations securely, we will utilize the open-source AGV Easy Install manager on a provisioned Cloudtop Linux VM. This gives us a seamless, interactive menu to securely deploy the official binaries."
**Visual:** A progressive path (Provision VM -> AGV Easy Install -> Initialize Workspace -> Verify Toolchain -> Test Agent).

**Talking Track:** "In this session, we focus entirely on our L100 goal: 'Getting the software installed and verified.' Future sessions will cover navigating the console, building your first app, and configuring advanced agent skills."
